terms.def: Malignancy of the prostate gland; the most common non-skin cancer in men.
Prostate cancer confined to the prostate, with no detectable spread.
Rising PSA after definitive therapy without imaging-detectable disease.
Metastatic Hormone-Sensitive Prostate Cancer — disease that still responds to androgen deprivation.
Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer — progresses despite androgen deprivation therapy.
Tumor / Node / Metastasis — standard cancer staging system describing primary tumor, lymph node involvement, and distant metastases.
Limited number of metastases (typically ≤3–5), often considered for targeted local therapy.
Prostate-Specific Antigen — blood biomarker used to screen, stage, and monitor prostate cancer activity.
Prostate-Specific Membrane Antigen — protein highly expressed on prostate cancer cells and the molecular target for PSMA imaging and theranostics.
Androgen Receptor and its splice variant AR-V7; AR-V7 is associated with resistance to androgen receptor-targeted therapies.
Positron Emission Tomography — functional/molecular imaging that detects gamma rays from positron-emitting radiotracers.
Computed Tomography — anatomical X-ray-based cross-sectional imaging.
Magnetic Resonance Imaging — soft tissue imaging using magnetic fields and radio waves.
Single Photon Emission Computed Tomography — nuclear imaging using gamma-emitting tracers.
Hybrid scanner combining functional PET with anatomical CT — the standard modality for PSMA imaging.
Hybrid scanner combining PET with MRI — used in select centers for higher soft-tissue contrast.
Whole-Body PET — imaging from skull base to mid-thigh (or eyes-to-thighs) for systemic disease assessment.
Standardized Uptake Value — normalized measure of radiotracer uptake corrected for injected dose and patient weight.
Maximum SUV in a lesion (single hottest voxel) — most commonly reported quantitative metric.
Mean SUV across a defined region of interest.
Mean SUV in a small fixed-size sphere (typically 1 cm³) around the hottest voxel — less noise-sensitive than SUVmax.
Metabolic Tumor Volume — total volume of metabolically active tumor.
Total Lesion Glycolysis (FDG) or Total Lesion Uptake (PSMA analog) — MTV multiplied by SUVmean.
Aggregate volumetric or uptake measure summed across all detected lesions.
Gallium-68 labeled PSMA tracer for PET imaging. Brand names include Locametz (Novartis) and Illuccix (Telix).
Pylarify (Lantheus) — FDA-approved fluorine-18 PSMA tracer with longer half-life than Ga-68 variants.
Fluorine-18 PSMA tracer with delayed renal clearance, useful for pelvic imaging.
Posluma (Blue Earth Diagnostics) — FDA-approved fluorine-18 PSMA tracer.
Fluorodeoxyglucose — general oncology PET tracer measuring glucose metabolism (not PSMA-specific).
Therapy + diagnostics paired pathway: an imaging tracer identifies a molecular target, and a matched therapeutic delivers radiation to that same target.
Radioligand Therapy — systemic targeted radiation delivered via a ligand that binds to tumor antigens.
Radiopharmaceutical Therapy — broader umbrella term often used interchangeably with RLT.
Pluvicto (Novartis) — FDA-approved Lutetium-177 PSMA radioligand therapy for mCRPC.
Actinium-225 PSMA — alpha-emitting radioligand therapy, currently investigational.
Radionuclide emitting beta particles (e.g., Lu-177) — longer tissue range, lower linear energy transfer (LET).
Radionuclide emitting alpha particles (e.g., Ac-225) — short range, very high LET, more cytotoxic per decay.
Response Evaluation Criteria In Solid Tumors — anatomical (CT/MRI) response framework based on lesion size changes.
PET Response Criteria In Solid Tumors — quantitative PET-based response framework using SUL changes.
Prostate Cancer Working Group response criteria; PCWG4 is the latest update incorporating PSMA imaging.
Response Evaluation Criteria In PSMA-PET/CT — emerging PSMA-specific response framework.
Following individual lesions across imaging timepoints to characterize response heterogeneity (TrackPSMA's approach).
Differences in interpretation between different readers (Nucs AI's preferred term over 'subjectivity').
The same reader's interpretation differing across separate reads of the same case.
Preferred terms for non-tumor uptake on PSMA imaging (avoid 'benign uptake').
Delineating tumor or organ boundaries on imaging — manual or automated.
AI-automated segmentation of tumors or organs.
Time required for a clinician to complete an imaging report — a key efficiency metric.
Quantitative tumor burden measurement summed across the entire scan.

Nucs AI's tumor detection, TNM staging, and whole-body tumor burden quantification on PSMA PET/CT. Reported metrics: 95% tumor detection, 87% TNM staging, 99% whole-body quantification, 70% reduction in reporting time.
Nucs AI's pre-therapy responder/non-responder identification for PSMA RLT. 94% NPV; multi-center validated in US/EU.
Nucs AI's standardized response assessment with lesion-by-lesion tracking across imaging timepoints.
Phase III trial that established efficacy of Lu-177-PSMA-617 in mCRPC and led to Pluvicto's FDA approval.
Australian randomized trial comparing Lu-177-PSMA-617 vs cabazitaxel in mCRPC.
Trial of Lu-177-PSMA-617 in pre-chemotherapy mCRPC.
Australian trial that established PSMA PET/CT superiority over conventional imaging for primary staging.
Trials evaluating PSMA PET/CT in primary diagnosis and risk stratification of prostate cancer.
Study referenced internally regarding PSMA PET use around biopsy decisions.
Software as a Medical Device — software that performs medical functions without being part of a hardware device.
FDA premarket notification pathway demonstrating substantial equivalence to a legally marketed predicate device.
FDA pathway for novel low-to-moderate-risk devices that lack a predicate.
Premarket Approval — most rigorous FDA pathway, required for Class III devices.
Investigational Device Exemption — FDA mechanism allowing clinical study of an unapproved device.
Humanitarian Device Exemption — FDA pathway for devices treating rare conditions.
FDA Quality System Regulation (QSR) governing medical device manufacturers.
Quality Management System Regulation — FDA's update aligning 21 CFR 820 with ISO 13485 (effective 2026).
International quality management system standard for medical device organizations.
European Medical Device Regulation governing medical devices placed on the EU market.
EU In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (2017/746) — counterpart to MDR for IVDs.
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — US patient health data privacy law.
General Data Protection Regulation — EU data protection and privacy law.
Institutional Review Board — ethics committee overseeing human subjects research.
Electronic Quality Management System (Nucs AI uses Greenlight Guru).
Design History File — compilation of records describing the design history of a finished device.
Device Master Record — documentation defining the procedures and specifications for a finished device.
Device History Record — record of production history for each unit of a finished device.
Standard Operating Procedure — documented process governing a recurring activity.
Form — controlled work form (e.g., FRM-07 supplier evaluation, FRM-08 job description).
Job Description — QMS-controlled document defining role responsibilities and qualifications.
Supplier Evaluation — QMS-controlled assessment of a supplier's capability and risk tier.
Non-Disclosure Agreement.
Data Processing Agreement — required under GDPR when sharing personal data with processors.
Business Associate Agreement — required under HIPAA when sharing PHI with vendors.
Service Organization Control 2 — security, availability, and confidentiality audit framework.
International information security management system standard.
Key Opinion Leader — influential physician or researcher whose endorsement carries clinical weight.
Medical Advisory Board — Nucs AI's MAB includes Drs. Calais (UCLA), Ceci (IEO Milan), Becker (Rutgers), and Nawaz (AstraZeneca).
An agreement restricting press coverage until a specified release time.
Tying brand commentary or content to breaking industry news to ride existing media attention.
Opinion editorial published under a named author, typically in a trade or general publication.
Hard news suitable for a press release. Nucs AI categories: Product, Partners, Data, Growth, Talent.
Marketing/PR activity timed to a conference's hype cycle without physically attending.
Society of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging — primary US nuclear medicine professional society.
European Association of Nuclear Medicine — primary European nuclear medicine society.
Radiological Society of North America — largest annual radiology conference.
American Society of Clinical Oncology — major oncology conference.
European Society for Medical Oncology — major European oncology conference.
European Association of Urology.
American Urological Association.
Journal of Nuclear Medicine — flagship journal of SNMMI.
European Journal of Nuclear Medicine and Molecular Imaging — flagship EANM journal.
Clinical Nuclear Medicine.
Journal of Clinical Oncology — flagship ASCO journal.
European Urology — top-tier urology journal, EAU's flagship.
Lantheus subsidiary providing aPROMISE — auto-segmentation and PSMA reporting software.
Imaging AI portfolio that includes PSMA workflow tools.
Manufacturer of Pluvicto (Lu-177-PSMA-617) and Locametz — primary RLT pharma player in PSMA.
Manufacturer of Pylarify (18F-DCFPyL) and parent of Exini.
Manufacturer of Posluma (18F-rhPSMA-7.3).
Strategic partner in oncology pipeline development.
Picture Archiving and Communication System — digital system for storing, retrieving, and distributing medical images. Nucs AI integrates with +90% of PACS systems supporting cloud connectivity.
Electronic Health Record — digital patient record system used across healthcare institutions. Nucs AI's platform is designed for EHR integration alongside PACS.
Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine — the universal standard format for medical imaging data. All Nucs AI products process DICOM files.
synonyms: digital imaging and communications in medicine

terms.def: A machine learning architecture where the model learns directly from raw input to final output without separate hand-engineered processing steps. Nucs AI uses an end-to-end approach — a key differentiator vs. competitors.
Nucs AI's text-based clinical companion that empowers physicians to analyze PSMA-PET/CT scans, generate reports, answer software FAQs, and streamline everyday tasks.
Nucs AI's upcoming structured reporting product for standardized PSMA-PET/CT clinical reports. Pending FDA 510(k) pre-submission.
The ability to connect and transmit medical imaging data via cloud infrastructure, enabling remote AI analysis without on-premise hardware.
Software as a Service — subscription-based software delivery model. DeepPSMA offers SaaS and Pay Per Scan pricing.
Proportion of patients achieving ≥50% PSA reduction from baseline during treatment — a standard marker of RLT treatment response. PSMA-R patients showed 51% vs 6% for PSMA-NR.
PSA Progression-Free Survival — time from treatment start to first PSA progression event. Key secondary endpoint in PSMA RLT trials.
Time from treatment start to death from any cause — the gold-standard primary endpoint in oncology trials.
Statistical measure comparing the rate of an event (death, progression) between two groups. HR < 1 favors treatment. SelectPSMA non-responders: 4.56x HR for PSA progression, 2.1x for death.
Concordance index — measures a prognostic model's discrimination (0.5 = random, 1.0 = perfect). PSMA-VOL achieved 0.80 for OS in DeepPSMA validation.
Negative Predictive Value — probability that a negative test correctly identifies a true negative. SelectPSMA: 94% NPV for non-responder identification.
Positive Predictive Value — probability that a positive test correctly identifies a true positive.
Proportion of true positives correctly identified by a test. Measures ability to detect disease when present.
Proportion of true negatives correctly identified by a test. Measures ability to correctly rule out disease.
A measurable indicator that predicts disease outcome regardless of treatment. PSMA-VOL demonstrated strong prognostic value for overall survival.
A measurable indicator that predicts response to a specific treatment. SelectPSMA functions as a predictive biomarker for PSMA radioligand therapy.
PSMA-positive tumor volume — total volume of PSMA-avid tumor measured by DeepPSMA. C-index 0.80 as OS prognosticator.
SelectPSMA's binary classification: PSMA Responders (PSMA-R) vs Non-Responders (PSMA-NR) to PSMA RLT. PSMA-R: 51% PSA50 response vs 6% for PSMA-NR.
A study that analyzes previously collected data. DeepPSMA's initial validation and SelectPSMA's Johns Hopkins study were retrospective.
A study that enrolls patients and collects data going forward. SelectPSMA's multi-center US/EU validation (JCO 2025) was prospective.
A study conducted across multiple clinical sites. Increases generalizability. DeepPSMA validated across 6 centers (EU + US).
Demonstrating that a medical device or software performs as intended in a clinical setting with real patient data.
Nucs AI's early-access clinical partner program — hospitals that validate, shape, and co-develop products with early access, research visibility, and real-world impact.
Computer-Aided Detection — FDA classification for AI that assists in detecting abnormalities. DeepPSMA's CADe 510(k) is planned after LLZ approval.
Computer-Aided Diagnosis/Characterization — FDA classification for AI that assists in characterizing or classifying detected abnormalities.
Lesion-Level Analysis — FDA study type for standalone model performance validation. DeepPSMA's first 510(k) submission targets LLZ approval.
Multi-Reader Multi-Case — FDA clinical performance study design where multiple readers interpret the same cases with and without AI assistance.
Formal meeting with FDA before a regulatory submission to discuss study design, intended use, and classification. Also called pre-sub.
Total Addressable Market — the total revenue opportunity if 100% market share is captured. Medical imaging AI: $25B by 2033; prostate-specific: $3.5B.
Serviceable Addressable Market — the segment of TAM reachable with current products and go-to-market. Nucs AI's SAM: ~$2.5B (US, EU-5, select APAC).
Serviceable Obtainable Market — the realistic portion of SAM a company can capture. Nucs AI targeting $375M annual revenue by ~2033.
Contract Research Organization — company hired to conduct clinical trials on behalf of pharma/medtech. Market segment: $230M for Nucs AI.
Insurance company or government entity that reimburses healthcare costs. Nucs AI's payer segment: $250M, entering via health economics pilots.
Original Equipment Manufacturer — scanner and device manufacturers (GE, Siemens, Philips) who could embed Nucs AI's technology into their products.
An FDA-regulated diagnostic test or device linked to a specific therapy to identify patients most likely to benefit. SelectPSMA's CDx submission is planned for 2027.
Current Procedural Terminology code — used for medical billing and reimbursement. Obtaining a CPT code for SelectPSMA is a key commercial milestone.
An early-stage demonstration that a technology works as intended. AstraZeneca completed a successful PoC with Nucs AI.
Initial Public Offering — when a private company first offers shares on a public stock exchange. A potential exit pathway for Nucs AI.
The observable clinical characteristics of a patient — including symptoms, history, and functional status — that inform diagnosis and treatment context.
Hormonal treatment that suppresses androgen levels to slow prostate cancer growth. Foundational therapy before mCRPC progression.
Systemic cancer treatment using taxane drugs (docetaxel, cabazitaxel). Standard treatment line for mCRPC alongside or before RLT.
Cancer treatment that harnesses the immune system to target tumor cells. An expanding treatment modality in Nucs AI's vision beyond RLT.
Terbium-149 — an emerging alpha-emitting radionuclide for targeted alpha therapy. Part of Nucs AI's expansion vision beyond Lu-177.
Lead-212 — an emerging beta/alpha-emitting radionuclide for targeted radiopharmaceutical therapy.
Six-Transmembrane Epithelial Antigen of Prostate 2 — a molecular target for next-generation PSMA-like imaging and therapy beyond PSMA itself.
Circulating tumor DNA — tumor-derived DNA fragments in blood. A liquid biomarker that Nucs AI's vision integrates alongside imaging data.
Somatostatin Receptor-Targeted PET/CT — molecular imaging modality for neuroendocrine tumors using DOTATATE/DOTATOC tracers.
HER2-targeted PET imaging — molecular imaging modality for HER2-expressing cancers (breast, gastric). Part of Nucs AI's future modality expansion.
Multiparametric MRI — MRI protocol combining multiple sequences (T2, DWI, DCE) for prostate cancer detection and characterization. Part of Nucs AI's modality expansion to PET + SPECT + mpMRI.
Study of an organism's complete set of DNA. A complementary data layer in Nucs AI's multimodal precision medicine vision.
Large-scale study of proteins expressed by cells or tissues. A future data layer in Nucs AI's multimodal platform.
Biomarkers derived from blood or other body fluids (ctDNA, circulating tumor cells, protein markers). A scalable complementary layer to imaging in Nucs AI's vision.
Fibroblast Activation Protein Inhibitor — an emerging PET tracer targeting tumor stroma across multiple cancer types. Broader applicability than PSMA.
Molecular Imaging TNM — a staging approach using molecular imaging (PSMA-PET/CT) data for TNM classification. DeepPSMA achieves 87% miTNM staging accuracy.
Bayer/Lantheus product (formerly Siemens Healthineers AI) for PSMA-PET quantification — a Nucs AI competitor. Acquired by Bayer in 2024.
MIM Software's nuclear medicine quantification and analysis platform — a Nucs AI competitor in general imaging workflow.
AI company providing tissue-based predictive analytics for prostate cancer using pathology images. SelectPSMA competitor in therapy prediction.
Exact Sciences' Genomic Prostate Score — genomic test predicting prostate cancer aggressiveness post-surgery. Competes with SelectPSMA in therapy biomarker space.
Veracyte's genomic biopsy classifier for prostate cancer prognosis post-surgery. Competes in biomarker space.
Radiopharmaceutical company developing next-generation targeted alpha therapies. Potential future pharma partner.
Radiopharmaceutical company (Illuccix/PSMA-11 manufacturer). Ongoing performance testing partnership with Nucs AI.
Major medical imaging OEM. Ongoing performance testing partnership with Nucs AI.
Imaging management and reader services company. Partner in Nucs AI's ecosystem.
Pharma company with radiopharmaceutical portfolio (acquired TRAQinform IQ from Lantheus). Operates in same PSMA-RLT space.
Major medical imaging OEM and health technology company. Potential strategic buyer or platform partner.
A defined group of patients in a clinical study sharing a common characteristic (e.g., Cohort A: initial staging, Cohort B: biochemical recurrence in DeepPSMA's FDA study).
Lutetium-177 labeled DOTATATE — FDA-approved RLT for somatostatin receptor-positive GEP-NETs. The other major approved theranostic alongside Pluvicto.
Radium-223 dichloride — FDA-approved alpha-emitting radiopharmaceutical for bone-metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.
Systemic therapy using I-131 for thyroid cancer and hyperthyroidism — one of the oldest and most established nuclear medicine therapies.
Radiopharmaceutical therapy using alpha-emitting radionuclides for highly localized, high-LET cell killing. The next frontier beyond beta-emitting RLT.
Peptide Receptor Radionuclide Therapy — RLT targeting somatostatin receptors on neuroendocrine tumors, typically using Lu-177-DOTATATE.
The measurement and calculation of radiation dose absorbed by organs and tumors during radiopharmaceutical therapy.
Gallium-68 labeled DOTATATE — PET tracer for imaging somatostatin receptor-positive neuroendocrine tumors.
Gallium-68 labeled DOTATOC — alternative somatostatin receptor PET tracer.
Fluorine-18 labeled amino acid analog PET tracer for prostate cancer recurrence detection. Being superseded by PSMA tracers.
Fluorine-18 sodium fluoride — bone PET tracer for detecting skeletal metastases.
Technetium-99m — the most widely used radioisotope in nuclear medicine.
Yttrium-90 — beta-emitting radioisotope used in radioembolization for liver tumors.
Iodine-131 — beta/gamma-emitting radioisotope used therapeutically and diagnostically.
Imaging performed specifically to plan, guide, or monitor radiopharmaceutical therapy.
A correction applied to PET/SPECT images to account for photon absorption by body tissues.
Standardized Uptake Value normalized to lean body mass. Used in PERCIST criteria.
The fundamental imaging device for SPECT and planar nuclear medicine.
A scanner's ability to distinguish two closely spaced objects — a key performance metric.
The degree to which measured imaging values reflect true radiotracer concentrations.
Visual assessment scale for FDG-PET treatment response in lymphoma (1–5 rating).
The amount of radiation energy deposited per unit mass of tissue (measured in Gray).
A plot of radiotracer concentration in a tissue or organ over time.
The cumulated radioactivity in a source organ divided by the administered activity.
The pattern of how a radiopharmaceutical distributes through the body after injection.
A class of tumors arising from neuroendocrine cells, often expressing somatostatin receptors.
